title: Pediatric diarrhea follow-up

red_flags.text: Prioritises red flags, dehydration, severe alternate illness, age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, neurological abnormality, and instability.
red_flags.rationale: Supports dehydration, fever, bleeding, vomiting, neurological and instability warnings for child diarrhoea follow-up; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Prioritises red flags, dehydration, severe alternate illness, age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, neurological abnormality, and instability.

red_flags.rationale: Supports worsening, caregiver and face-to-face referral context for child diarrhoea follow-up; no decision is generated.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Lists age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, dehydration, neurological, instability, caregiver, and worsening criteria for face-to-face assessment.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Age, onset, stool frequency and character, vomiting, intake, urine, fever, blood, exposure, examination, diagnosis and clinician plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for child diarrhoea follow-up.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
